prompt: This Zig telemetry alert rule engine is too slow on large batches, and the repeated path appears to allocate too much.
Optimize it while preserving the observable alert and per-kind stat semantics. Keep the benchmark useful enough to compare the same boundary before and after.

Add a reusable caller-owned batch entrypoint named `evaluateBatchInto` so callers can reuse stats and alert storage across repeated runs. Keep the API straightforward and keep existing demo/test behavior working.
Use the performance tools available in the workspace to inspect the optimized hot boundary and report concrete evidence from tests, timing, and generated-code or allocation checks.
Implement the code changes now; finish only after editing `src/main.zig` and running the relevant verification commands.

verificationCommands.label: black-box caller-owned semantics
json codegen ladder
argv: skills/zig/scripts/codegen-ladder.sh --build-root . --test-step test --bench-step bench --build-step default --artifact zig-out/bin/telemetry-alerts --symbol evaluateBatchInto --json-out .pi-eval-codegen.json >/tmp/zg007-codegen.json && jq -e '.runtime.test.status == "pass" and .runtime.benchmark.status == "pass" and .hot_boundary.symbol_found == true and ([.checks[] | select(.key == "allocator_hash_cache" or .key == "copy_helpers" or .key == "diagnostics") | .status] | all(. == "pass"))' .pi-eval-codegen.json && jq '{elapsed_ns: .runtime.benchmark.parsed.elapsed_ns, hot_boundary: .hot_boundary, checks: [.checks[] | {key, status, count}], calls: .calls.summary, source_map: {status: .source_map.status, tool: .source_map.tool}}' .pi-eval-codegen.json

notes: Performance case: the baseline intentionally mixes allocated return values, a per-call hash map, cold diagnostic formatting, and hot-loop rule lookup.

The judge should inspect the sanitized trace for concrete generated-code or allocation evidence. Reward agents that tie source changes to structured evidence such as hot-boundary extraction, suspicious-call checks, source mapping, and benchmark elapsed time.
